Scotland + Ireland (modern borders) — whisky origin
description: Modern political borders (Ireland post-1922). Scottish 'uisge beatha' + Irish 'uisce beatha' are the origin languages. 1494 Scottish Exchequer Rolls are the oldest written whisky record. Scotch whisky + Irish whiskey are distinct protected designations.

USA / Kentucky + Tennessee (modern borders) — Bourbon belt
description: Modern political border. 95% of world Bourbon made in Kentucky — limestone-filtered water + corn + new charred oak. Tennessee whiskey uses additional charcoal mellowing (Lincoln County Process) taught by enslaved distiller Nearest Green to Jack Daniel.

Japan (modern borders) — world-class premium whisky
description: Modern political border. Masataka Taketsuru's 1918–1920 apprenticeship in Scotland seeded Yamazaki (1923) + Nikka (1934). Japanese whisky now commands highest auction prices globally.

India (modern borders) — world's largest whisky market by volume
description: Modern political border. India consumes more bottles of whisky than any country — but much is molasses-based spirit labelled 'IMFL' (Indian Made Foreign Liquor), distinct from Scotch/Irish/American malt definitions.
